features.name: DataFrame Creation and I/O
features.description: Demonstrates creating DataFrames from CSV, JSON, objects, columns, and raw bytes, as well as saving, loading, comparing, appending, joining, and linking tables.

features.name: Column Operations
features.description: Shows how to add, access, type-convert, sort, and manipulate DataFrame columns, including virtual columns and image columns.

features.name: Row Filtering and Bitsets
features.description: Covers row selection, bitset boolean operations (AND/OR/XOR), aggregation, grouping, iterable rows, and collaborative filter-state synchronisation.

features.name: Metadata and Semantic Types
features.description: Demonstrates tagging columns and DataFrames with metadata, detecting and assigning semantic types, qualified numbers, value-matchers, and custom category ordering.

features.name: Grid Cell Rendering
features.description: Shows how to implement custom cell renderers, HTML cells, sparklines, format formulas, tooltips, hit-testing, and paint callbacks for the main data grid.

features.name: Grid Column Configuration
features.description: Covers column width, visibility, ordering, color-coding, styles, data formatting, controlled values, and scroll positioning in the data grid.

features.name: Built-in Viewers
features.description: Samples for every built-in Datagrok viewer: scatter plot, bar/box/line/pie chart, histogram, heat map, trellis, network diagram, globe, markup, form, statistics, and more.

features.name: Viewer Interaction and Customisation
features.description: Covers dynamic viewer creation, selection/filter/highlight coordination, custom scatter-plot rendering, filter widgets, and viewer event handling.

members.name: viewer-data-events
members.description: Handling viewer events: onDataEvent, onDataSelected, onDataHovered, onDataRowClicked.

features.name: View Management and Navigation
features.description: Shows how to open, navigate, and manage application views, including docking, ribbons, toolbox pages, layouts, layout permissions, and virtual views.

features.name: UI Widgets
features.description: A catalogue of atomic UI controls: buttons, menus, dropdowns, markdown, icons, images, progress indicators, sliders, tabs, and tooltips.

features.name: Layout Containers
features.description: Shows the block, box, panel, flexbox, splitter, and widget-tree layout primitives used to arrange content inside a Datagrok view.

features.name: Dialogs and Input Forms
features.description: Covers building modal dialogs, wizards, input forms, combo inputs, custom inputs, parameter validation, and master-detail forms using the JS API.

features.name: Event Handling
features.description: Shows how to subscribe to and prevent platform events: table, layout, function, and UI events.

features.name: Function Registry
features.description: Covers registering, discovering, calling, and caching registered functions; attaching handlers, markup handlers, and auto-renderers; and managing init hooks.

features.name: Package Scripting
features.description: Shows how to write multi-language scripts (Python, R, JS) within a package, access file storage from scripts, inspect the scripting context, and handle errors.

members.name: Pearson
members.description: Computes Pearson correlation for two numerical columns

features.name: Server API (dapi)
features.description: Shows how to use grok.dapi to save/load entities, fetch external URLs, manage user groups, inspect the current user, and persist user settings.

features.name: Projects and Spaces
features.description: Shows how to list, open, and navigate projects and spaces via the JS API.

features.name: File Management
features.description: Demonstrates reading files, checking file existence, and accessing the platform file store from JS scripts.

features.name: Database Queries
features.description: Shows ad-hoc, parameterized, and dynamic SQL queries against Postgres and MSSQL connections, including in-place DB editing and master-detail query patterns.

features.name: Cheminformatics
features.description: Samples for the Chem JS API: molecule rendering, substructure search (SMARTS/molfile/ALS), sketcher, R-group decomposition, MCS, diversity search, and scaffold alignment.

features.name: Machine Learning
features.description: Covers ML API samples: PCA, clustering, missing-value imputation, predictive model training, random data generation, and statistical correlation.

members.name: Pearson
members.description: Computes Pearson correlation for two numerical columns

features.name: Application Demos
features.description: Full mini-application examples (stock-broker, bioreactor, general demo) that wire together multiple API surfaces into a cohesive interactive workflow.
